text:
There once was a wise old owl named Oscar. Oscar lived high up in a towering oak tree. Every morning he would whistle a cheerful tune. One day, a curious squirrel named Sparkle came into his forest cabin.

Sparkle: Good morning, Oscar!

Oscar: Good morning, little squirrel! What's on your agenda today?

Sparkle: Oh, nothing much. I've been running all over the place trying to collect acorns.

Oscar: That'll do nicely!

One quiet afternoon, Sparkle heard a strange noise. It wasn't a whistle. It wasn't Oakie Oldwl ossywhoooo.

Just then a shadow crossed the oak's leaves. It was a boy. He was skinny, with glasses askew and a big old book.

Sparkle: Whoa! Are you lost?

The boy glanced around nervously.

Boy: Uh, yeah. I could use some help. That book? Physics.

Oscar: Oh dear. We'll guide you
